data.narrative:
Sift is a Series B–stage space and aerospace company founded by Karthik Gollapudi and Austin Spiegel, currently valued at $274M post-money following $65M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers at the same or adjacent stage and 5 public space & aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), with private comparables including Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Aalyria ($1.3B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage transitions, yielding $521M. Peer-median analysis across 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies produces a $1.2B midpoint, with peer valuations ranging from $400M to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue disclosure for the target. These two methods generate an implied low of $443M, median of $873M, and high of $1.4B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $443M to $1.4B, with a central estimate near $873M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty in whether Sift's traction more closely tracks earlier-stage peers at the range floor or approaches mid-stage peers commanding $1B+ valuations. Revenue and customer concentration data would materially narrow this band.
